questions.question: What is the 'Delta Log' and what fundamental role does it play in a Delta Lake table?
options: It is a monitoring dashboard for tracking Spark job performance.

It is an ordered series of JSON transaction journal files stored alongside the Parquet data files. Every DML operation (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) writes a new JSON entry recording which files were added and removed, providing the foundation for ACID guarantees and Time Travel.
It is a binary index file for accelerating column predicate pushdown.
It is the Databricks Cloud UI for browsing Delta tables visually.
questions.answer: It is an ordered series of JSON transaction journal files stored alongside the Parquet data files. Every DML operation (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) writes a new JSON entry recording which files were added and removed, providing the foundation for ACID guarantees and Time Travel.
questions.explanation: The Delta Log is analogous to a database's Write-Ahead Log (WAL). It's the authoritative record of everything that ever happened to the table, enabling recovery and time travel.

questions.question: Why is the MERGE (Upsert) operation critical for building CDC (Change Data Capture) pipelines into a Delta Lake table?
options: MERGE compresses all existing Parquet files when executed.

CDC pipelines receive a stream of changes (new rows, updated rows, deleted rows). MERGE handles all three cases in a single atomic operation — updating existing matching rows and inserting new ones — without requiring expensive DELETE + INSERT workarounds.
MERGE allows multiple Spark clusters to write to the same table simultaneously.
MERGE automatically generates surrogate keys for dimension tables.
questions.answer: CDC pipelines receive a stream of changes (new rows, updated rows, deleted rows). MERGE handles all three cases in a single atomic operation — updating existing matching rows and inserting new ones — without requiring expensive DELETE + INSERT workarounds.
questions.explanation: Without MERGE (upsert), you would need to read the entire target table, join with source changes, and rewrite everything — a massively expensive operation on a Petabyte-scale table.

questions.question: What performance optimization does the 'Change Data Feed (CDF)' feature in Delta Lake provide for downstream consumers?
options: It compresses the Delta Log JSON files into a binary format.

Instead of downstream jobs re-reading and re-processing the entire table on every run to detect changes, CDF exposes only the row-level changes (inserts, updates, deletes) that occurred since the last read, reducing compute costs for incremental processing dramatically.
It automatically triggers downstream Airflow DAGs when a table changes.
It replicates the Delta table to multiple AWS regions simultaneously.
questions.answer: Instead of downstream jobs re-reading and re-processing the entire table on every run to detect changes, CDF exposes only the row-level changes (inserts, updates, deletes) that occurred since the last read, reducing compute costs for incremental processing dramatically.
questions.explanation: Without CDF, a downstream Spark job must scan all 10TB to find the 1,000 changed rows. With CDF, it only reads the 1,000 changed rows directly from the change log.
